title: Code Review — The Gentle Art
philosophy: Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu (BJJ) translates to 'the gentle art.' When we roll with our training partners, the goal is not destruction, but mutual growth. Code review is our digital tatami mat; we test each other's code to find weaknesses before the real world does.
economics: Quality Assurance (QA) prevents negative externalities. Reviewing code ensures that technical debt is not offloaded onto the future community or end-users.
techconcept: On platforms like GitHub, we propose our code changes for review before they are merged into the main project.
lesson: A good review asks: is this change correct, clear, and kind to future maintainers? Proposing changes through a review process distributes responsibility and makes systems more resilient.
challenge: What is the two-word term for proposing a merge so peers can review your code on GitHub?
hint: It starts with 'pull' and ends with 'request'.
